hints.Ioannes: 'Ioannes' is a proper noun, the name John

hints.to eat: That would be 'edere' or 'esse' (different verb with different conjugation)

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the principal parts of 'edo' (to eat)
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the principal parts of 'sto' (to stand)
hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are the principal parts of 'eo' (to go)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'Ioannem'; 'Ioannes' is the subject here
hints.vocative: Vocative is for direct address; here John is being named, not addressed

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'Ioannes' has genitive 'Ioannis'
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um; 'Ioannes' has genitive 'Ioannis'
hints.indeclinable: 'Ioannes' does decline: Ioannes, Ioannis, Ioanni, Ioannem, Ioanne

hints.accusative: 'Nomen' is a neuter noun, so nominative and accusative are identical; here it is predicate nominative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'nomen' is a 3rd declension neuter
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension neuters end in -um; 'nomen' has genitive 'nominis'
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have -us in the genitive; 'nomen' has genitive 'nominis'
